comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text: When MCP tool returns isError (e.g., google_search missing creds, retrieve_and_generate missing KB env), langchain_mcp_adapters raises ToolException and CLI crashes (seen with OpenAI and Ollama). Should catch tool exceptions and return a normal assistant message + log.

comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text: langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite.aio not available in current langgraph version; CLI failed. I added fallback to MemorySaver so runs, but persistence for pause/resume is lost. Need to add proper sqlite checkpointer package or adjust version.

comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text: Installed langgraph-checkpoint-sqlite and added checkpoint config. create_checkpointer now uses sqlite path from model.json (checkpoint.path) and falls back to MemorySaver if unavailable.

comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text:
Test results (Feb 3, 2026):
- OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini): non-tool chat works. current_time tool works (but cached timestamp reused within TTL). google_search tool fails with ToolException: Missing Google CSE credentials (despite envs set), causing CLI crash/trace.
- Bedrock (anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620-v1:0): non-tool chat works. current_time tool works w/ citations. google_search returns model message about missing Google CSE credentials; no crash.
- Ollama (llama3.3:70b): non-tool chat works only when --no-tools. With tools_policy=auto, model invoked retrieve_and_generate and crashed: Missing BEDROCK_KNOWLEDGE_BASE_ID. With tools_policy=planner, current_time tool works and returns unix timestamp.
Notes: Tool errors from MCP (google_search/retrieve_and_generate) can crash CLI for OpenAI/Ollama via ToolException. Tool caching makes current_time return stale value within TTL.

comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text: Agent smoke conversation (OpenAI, Feb 3 2026): 1) time question answered with local timestamp; 2) NVIDIA price question answered using web search; 3) follow-up asking for page snippet returned 'couldn't extract any snippets' (no snippet available).

comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text: current_time tool result is cached (TTL 300s) so time responses repeat; should bypass cache for volatile tools or allow per-tool cache settings.

title: MCP tool server does not see Google CSE/Scrapfly envs

comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text: google_search/internet_search failed with 'Missing Google CSE credentials' even though env vars are set in parent process. Likely MCP server process not inheriting env or langchain_mcp_adapters requires explicit env in mcp_servers.json. Needs fix/verification.

comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text: Fixed by injecting allowlisted env vars into stdio MCP server config (resolve_mcp_config). Re-tested: google_search now works for OpenAI + Bedrock.

comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text: With tools_policy=auto, llama3.3:70b invoked retrieve_and_generate even when asked for current_time; caused ToolException due to missing Bedrock KB env. Planner policy works. Consider defaulting Ollama to planner or filtering tools per provider.

comments.author: vinaye1
comments.text: Implemented ollama default: if model_provider=ollama and tools_policy=auto, system uses planner.
